Add the chunks of grilled chicken and simmer for 5 minutes longer. Evenly divide the chunks of chicken with the sauce onto 4 heated plates. Garnish the dish with sliced almonds and coriander leaves.

name: Murgh Makhani IV (Velvet Butter Chicken)
instructions:
1. Combine 2 T. yogurt with 1 t. each tandoori and garam masalas, cinnamon, clove and cumin powders, and salt.  Marinate 4 lb. chicken breasts (diced) for 1 hour.
2.  Sauté the chicken breasts until well done.
3.  Saut
2 chopped onions till golden, then add 2 T. ginger-garlic paste and sauté until the oil separates.
4.  Add 3 T. ground  cashews, one groundchili pd, 2 tomatos ground into a paste and cook for 10 min.
5.  Add the yogurt mixture, 1 T. whipped cream and the chicken. Mix well and cook till done.  Garnish with finely chopped coriander leaves.

name: Summary of Murgh Makhani recipes for 4 lb. chicken breasts:
instructions:
10 T butter, 1.5 C heavy cream
1/3 pt yogurt, 3 oz butter, 1/3 pt sour cream
1.5 T yogurt,
1.5 C cream,
4 T yogurt,
1 stick butter, 1 T whipping cream
4 t cumin, 4 green chiles (whole)
6 green cardamom, bay leaf, saffron
1 t cumin, 2 sauteed onions

name: Lamb Chops and Raita (serves four)
instructions:
1.  In a medium bowl, combine a 3-in. piece of seedless cucumber (peeled and diced), 4 minced scallions, 1 T. fresh lemon juice, 1 C. plain yogurt, 1 T. extra-virgin olive oil, 1 T. finely chopped fresh dill, 1/8 T. paprika, kosher salt and pepper to taste.  Mix well.
2.  In a small bowl, whisk together 1 T. fresh lemon juice, 3 T. extra-virgin olive oil, 2 T. finely chopped fresh oregano, 2 t. minced garlic, 1/2 t. kosher salt & 1/4 t. pepper.  Brush the mixture onto 8 double-cut rib lambchops (1-1.25 in. thick, trimmed).
3.  Grill the lambchops directly over medium heat, turning once (~ 5 min on a side).  Serve warm with the sauce.

name: Indian Mint Sauce (makes eight)
instructions:
1. Blend 1/3 C. white vinegar, 2 T. sugar, 1/2 t. salt, 2 hot green chiles (stemmed and seeded), 1/2 in. sliced ginger, and 1.8 t. toasted cumin for 1 min. in a food processor.
2.  Add 1/2 C. tightly packed coriander, 1/4 C. canned mango and 1/2 C. tightly packed mint leaves in smll portions, blending after each addition.  Serve in a small bowl.

name: Sesame Shrimp (serves six)
instructions:
1. Shell and devein 1 lb. of medium to large raw shrimp - wash thoroughly.  Pat dry, place in a mixing bowl and add 1 t. minced garlic, 1 t. grated ginger, 1 t. cayenne, 1/2 t. salt and 1 t. cornstarch.  Mix well.
2.  Combine 2/3 C. toasted sesame seeds and 1.5 t. nigella seeds (kalonji).  Dip shrimp one at a time into the seeds, coating lightly.  Arrange shrimp in a 10-in. microwave-proof pie plate, tails toward the center, and cover.  Microwave for 1 min. on high, until shrimp are just done.  Sprinkle with lemon juice and serve.

name: Tomato Dal Soup (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Wash 5 T. toovar daal beans (split and skinned) in several changes of water.  Cook at a bare simmer in 1.5 C. water in a saucepan for 40-45 min., stirring frequently during the last 15 min.
2.  Cook 1.5 t. mustard seeds and 1 fresh hot red serrano pepper in 1.5 T. ghee in a large saucepan over moderately high heat until seeds begin to pop.  Add 1.5 C. water, 1.5 lb. plum tomatoes (chopped), 2 T. finely chopped fresh ginger, 1 t. ground coriander, 1 t. tamarind concentrate, 3/4 t. turmeric, 1/2 t. cumin, 1/2 t. asafetida powder and 2 chopped jalape
os and bring to a boil.  Simmerwith occasional stirring for 10 min, then add the bean paste and 1 C. water.  Stir to incorporate and bring to a boil.  Season with salt and serve garnished with fresh cilantro.

name: Indian Lemon Rice I (serves 2)
instructions:
1.  Cook 1 C. basmati rice in 2 C. boiling water for 18 min.  Allow to cool.
2.  Warm 2 T. ghee, then season with 1 t. channa daal, 1/2 t. urad daal, 1 red chile, 1 t. dried mustard seed, and 1/4 t. heeng.  Remove from pan when the mustard seed begins to crackle.  Add 1 t. green chiles (deseeded) and 1/2 t. turmeric and mix with the cooled rice.
3.  Mix in 3-4 T. toasted cashew nuts, 2 T. roasted, powdered urad daal, juice from 1/2 lemon, and 4 curry leaves.  Mix well and garnish with a twist of lemon in the center.

name: Basmati Pilaf (serves six)
instructions:
1. Microwave 6 T. light vegetable oil in a large mixing bowl until very hot.  Add 12 green  or white cardamom pods (lightly crushed), three 3-in. pieces of cinnamon, 18 whole cloves, and six bay leaves.  Microwave uncovered another 3-4 min.  Add 1.5 t. minced garlic, 1 T. grated ginger and 2.25 C. finely chopped onion.  Microwave another 8 min, stirring once.
2.  Add 3 C. basmati rice and 6 C. chicken stock.  Stir, then microwave uncovered for 15-20 min, stirring twice.  Cover and cook another 6 min.  Let rest 5 min. then serve.

name: Spinach Paneer (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Bring 10 C. milk to a full boil in a heavy pot, stirring occasionally.  Reduce heat and slowly stir in 1/3 C. fresh lemon juice.  Cook until milk begins to separate (1-2 min.).  Remove pot from heat and allow to stand 10 min.
2.  Pour mixture into a colalnder lined with a triple layer of cheesecloth.  Rinse curds gently under warm, running water.  Gather edges of cheesecloth and twist together gently, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.  Transfer to a bowl and flatten into a disk.  Weight the cheese with a bowl of water and allow to stand 1 hr.  Pour off any liquid that has accumulated.
3.  Cut the paneer into 1/2-in. cubes and mix with 1/2 t. turmeric and 1.5 C. water.  Allow to stand 20 min. Mash 2 minced cloves of garlic and 2 t. fresh ginger (peeled and chopped) into a paste.
4.  Cook 2 bunches of spinach (stems removed) in 1/2 C. water until wilted and tender, approx. 2 min.  Puré e in a food processor with the water.
5.  Remove and brown the paneer in 6 T. ghee over high heat in two batches, turning gently ot avoid breaking up.  transfer to a large bowl.
6.  Blanch 1.5 C. pearl onions (peeled) and sauté in the ghee for 5 min.  Reduce to moderate heat and add the garlic/ginger paste, then cook another minute or so.  Add 2 T. ground coriander, 1 t. Indian red chile powder, 1 t. asafetida powder and 3-in. of cinnamon stick.  Cook until onions are tender, ~ 5 min.
7.  Peel and chop 4 plum tomatoes.  Add tomatoes and the spinach puré e and bring to a simmer with occasional stirring.  Cook until almost dry, then gently stir inot the paneer.  Salt to taste and serve.

name: Indian Lemon Rice II (serves 2)
instructions:
1.  Boil 2 C. water with 1/2 t. turmeric and 1 t. salt.
2.  Heat 1.5 T. ghee in a pan, saut
1 C. washed basmati rice until grain becomes translucent.  Add the boiling water, stir, cover and cook at a simmer 15 min.
3.  Melt 1/2 T. ghee with 1/2 t. mustard, 1 t. urad daal and 1/4 t. heeng.  Add 1/2 C. cashew nuts and cook 2 min and add to rice.  Fold into rice with the juice from 1 lemon.  Garnish with 1 t. cut dhania leaves and a twist of lemon in the center.

name: Avocado Raita (serves six)
instructions:
1. Mix 3 C. yogurt, 2 T. minced coriander, 1 t. toasted coriander seeds, 1 t. toasted cumin seeds, 1/4 t. black pepper and salt to taste.  Refrigerate for 2 hr.
2.  Mix in 2 peeled, sliced and seeded cucumbers, 1 peeled, seeded and chopped tomato, and 1 cubed avocado (sprinkled with lemon juice).  Top with paprika and serve.

name: Fried Rice with Basil  (from Thai Vegetarian Cooking)
instructions:
1.  Add 2 T. oil to a wok and stir-fry in order: 1 clove garlic (minced), 3 small red or green chilies (minced), 1 C. button mushrooms (halved), 1 small onion (minced), 2 C. cooked rice, 1 small bundle long beans (2oz) (1/2 inch pieces) (green beans can substitute), 1 small sweet red or green pepper.
2.  Add 1/2 t. sugar, 3 T. light soy sauce and15 sweet basil leaves.  Stri-fry another couple of munute and serve.

name: Beef Satay and Cucumber Pickles (serves four)
instructions:
1. Combine 1/4 C. white wine, 1/4 C. Thai fish sauce (nuoc mam), 1 T. fresh lime juice, 2-3 cloves chopped garlic.
2.  Slice 1 lb. chicken, flank steak or porkloin, and marinate in the above for 1-2 hr. in the refrigerator.  Place on skewers and grill or broil, turning once, until just done.
3.  Grind 1/3 C. shelled, unsalted roasted peanuts in a food processor.  Heat 1 T. peanut oil in a skillet.  Stir in 2 t. red curry paste, then whisk in 1 t. sugar, 1 C. unsweetened coconut milk.  Bring to a boil, whisking constantly.  Remove from heat and add 2 t. soy sauce and 1 T. fresh lime juice.  Serve with grilled meat from above.
4.  Combine 1/2 C. white vinegar, 1/2 C. water, 1/2 C. sugar and 1 t. salt in a small saucepan.  Bring to a boil, then remove from heat and cool to room temp.
5.  Peel and slice two large cucumbers, then combine with vinegar mixture.  Add 2 T. coarsely chopped purple onion and 1 t. minced fresh red chile pepper, stirring well.  Top with 1 T. minced peanuts and 1 T. chopped coriander.  Serve wit hsatay above.

name: Beef Satay and Szechuan Sauce
instructions:
1.  Soak and chill 24 bamboo skewers.  Cut a 3/4 lb. New York strip steak into 3"x1" strips.  Skewer each piece of meat length-wise, arrange on a platter and refrigerate.
2.  Combine 1/2 C. soy sauce, 1 T. honey, 1 t. cround cumin and 1/2 t. turmeric.  Marinate the meat in the soy sauce for 15 min at room temperature.
3.  Saut
2 T. minced garlic and one chopped green onion in 2 T.  butter unitl soft.  Add 1 C. chicken stock , 1/4 C. soy sauce and 1 t. (or more) chile flakes and cook an additional 2 in.  Strain and whisk in 4 T. melted butter.  Broil the meat for 30 sec on each side and serve with the sauce.

name: Drunkards Noodles (Thai Veg again)
instructions: 1.  Add 2 T. oil to a wok and stir-fry in order: 1 clove garlic (minced), 2 small red or green chilies, 4 oz Sen Yai noodles (broad flat rice noodles), 1 small onion (diced), 1 medium tomato (diced), 4 kaffir lime leaves, 6 sweet basil leaves, 1 t. dark soy sauce, 2 T. light soy sauce, 1/2 t. sugar, 1 small sweet red or green pepper (diced).

name: Spicy Mixed Vegetable Salad
instructions:
1.  Bring a large pot of water to a boil and blanch (individually): 1 oz chinese cabbage (one large leaf), 2 ears baby sweet corn (chopped into circles), 1 young carrot (chopped into matchsticks), 1/3 C. button mushrooms (finely sliced) and 1/3 C. black fungus mushrooms [cloud ears] (finely chopped).  Drain and place in a bowl.
2.  Add 1 stalk celery [stem and leaf] (finely chopped), 1 clove garlic (finely chopped), 4 small red or green chilies (finely chopped), 2 T. light soy sauce, 2 T. lemon juice, 1 t. sugar and 1 t. sesame seeds.  Toss well and garnish with 1 medium tomato and 10 mint leaves.

name: Pad Thai (from Thai Vegetarian Cooking)
instructions:
1.  Add 3 T. oil to a wok and stir-fry in order: 1 clove garlic (minced), 1/2 C. tofu (cubed), 1 egg, 4oz Sen Lek noodles (flat rice noodles), 1 T. perserved turnip, 2 scallions (minced), 1.5 T. roasted peanuts (chopped), 2 oz (mung) bean sprouts, 1/2 t. chili powder, 1 t. sugar, 2 T. light soy sauce, 1 T. lemon juice.
2.  Serve with 1/2 C. chopped peanuts and 1 oz. beansprouts (uncooked) as garnish.

name: Shrimp and noodles in peanut sauce (serves four)
instructions:
1. Grind 1/3 C. shelled, unsalted roast peanuts in a food processor.  Heat 1 T. peanut oil in a skillet.  Stir in 2 t. red curry paste, then whisk in 1 t. sugar, 1 C. unsweetened coconut milk.  Bring to a boil, whisking constantly.  Remove from heat and add 2 t. soy sauce and 1 T. fresh lime juice.
2.  Soak 12 oz. rice vermicelli in hot water for 10 min.  Drain and cook in boiling water until just tender.  Drain and rinse, then place in a large bowl and toss with the peanut sauce.  Mix in 1 lb. cooked and shelled baby shrimp, 12 thinly sliced scallions, 2 T. toasted sesame seeds and kosher salt to taste.  Divide into four portions and serve.

name: Hot and Sour Soup
instructions:
1.  Boil the following in 5 C. water until soft:  1 medium onion, 2 carrots, 2 stalks celery, 3-4 coriander roots, 1 t. peppercorns (all coarsely chopped).   Reduce to 3 C. in volume, strain out vegetables.
2.  Wrap 2 large green chilies, 4 small hot chilies, 2 cloves garlic and 2 small shallots in aluminum foil and broil until soft.  Grind into a paste in a mortar, then add 1 medium tomato (chopped), 1 round eggplant (chopped), 2 T. lemon juice, 1/2 t. salt and 1 t. sugar.  Mix well.  This is Nam Prik Pow sauce.
3.  Stir  1 t. sauce into vegetable stock, then add 1 inch lemon grass (chopped into rings), 3 kaffir lime leaves (torn in three), 2 T. light soy sauce, 1 t. sugar, 2 T. lemon juice, 2/3 C. oyster mushrooms and 2-3 fresh small chilies (chopped).  Simmer for at least 10 min.
4.  Garnish with coriander leaves and serve.

name: Pad Thai II  (serves four)
instructions: Soak 12 oz. rice noodles in cold water for 1 hr.  In a separate bowl,  whisk together 2 T. tamarind paste, 3 T. fish sauce, 3 T. sugar and 2 T. lime juice with 1/2 C. water.

name: Briefly steam 4 C broccoli florets and one carrot (sliced thin).
instructions:
Peel and devein 4 large raw shrimp.
Beat three eggs in a small bowl.  Also, mince 4 cloves  garlic and 2 t. ginger, and slice 4 scallions thinly.  Place 1/2 C. chopped peanuts in a bowl.  Chop 5 T. cilantro.  Ready 2 C. bean sprouts.
Drain the noodles, then heat 3/4 C. canola oilin a wok to almost smoking over high heat.  Add the garlic and ginger, and saut
30 sec.  Add the shrimp and sauté another 2-3 min.  Transfer to a plate.
Add the noodles to the wok and stir-fry 1 min.  Pour in 3/4 of the tamarind sauce and toss to coat the noodles.  Cook until noodles are al dente, then push them to one side and scramble the eggs.  Add the shrimp, broccoli, carrot, scallions, bean sprouts and half the peanuts.  Toss to mix, then divide among four plates and garnish with the remaining peanuts, cilantro, lime wedges, sesame seeds and red pepper flakes.

name: Fried Potato with ginger and mushroom sauce
instructions:
1.  Deep-fry 2-3 potatoes (cut into rounds) until golden brown.  Drain.
2.  Saut
1 t. garlic, then add 2 T. flour and cook for 1 min.  Add 2 T. water, then 1/2 sweet pepper (cut into strips), 2 scallions (1 inch sections), 4 T. light soy sauce, 1 t. sugar and 1/2 t. ground white pepper.
3.  Pour the vegetable mix over the potatoes and garnish with coriander.

name: Lemon Chicken (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Place four chicken breasts in a shallow dish.  Combine 2 T. soy sauce, 1/4 t. sesame oil, 1 t. salt and 1 T. gin (or vodka) and pour over chicken.  Toss to coat and allow to stand for 30 min.
2.  Drain and discard marinade.  Add chicken pieces to 3 eggwhites (beaten until frothy) and toss to coat.  Dredge the chicken in 1 C. water-chestnut flour.
3.  Preheat oven to 200 °F.  Brown the chicken in 1/2 in. deep peanut oil at 350

name: F in batches.  Drain.
instructions:
4.  Bring 3/4 C. sugar, 1/2 C. white vinegar, 1 C. chicken broth, 1 T. cornstarch (mixed with 2 T. water), 1 t. MSG and juice plus zest from one large lemon in a pan and stir until thickened.  Add 3 small carrots, 1/2 bell pepper and one small onion (all julienned), plus three rings of canned pineapple (shredded).
5.  Cut chicken into 1-in. slices crosswise and place on top of shredded1/4 head of iceberg lettuce on a serving platter, top with the sauce and serve immediately.

name: Chiang Mai Curry
instructions: 1.  Add 2 T. oil to a wok and stir-fry in order: 1 T. red curry paste, 1 C. pre-fried bean curd (chopped into 1 inch strips), 1/2 t. turmeric powder, 4 T. coconut milk, 1 C. chinese mushrooms [shitake], 1 T. lemon juice, 1 T. light soy sauce, 1 t. sugar, 1/2 t. salt, 1 inch ginger (matchsticks), 1 head pickled garlic (cut to make flowers?).  Serve with a garnish of sliced tomato.

name: Fried Aubergine with chili and basil in yellow bean sauce
instructions:
1.  Heat 2 T. oil and saut
1 t. garlic (chopped) and 3 small chilies (chopped finely).  Add 1 large purple eggplant (1 inch cubes) and 4 C. vegetable stock.  Simmer until soft.
2.  Add 1/2 sweet pepper, 1 t. yellow bean sauce, 1 T. light soy sauce, 1 t. sugar and 20 red basil leaves, simmer for a few minutes and serve.

name: Grilled lobster with Vietnamese Dipping Sauce (serves four)
instructions:
Combine 4 minced cloves of garlic, 4 minced asian red chiles, 3 T. sugar, 3 T. Vietnamese fish sauce (nuoc mam) and 3 T. fresh lime juice.  Set aside.
Steam 4 1.5-lb. lobsters for 5 min.  When cool enough to handle, twist off claws and tails.  Cut tails in half, lengthwise, and rub the exposed meat with 1 t. canola oil.  Grill claws and tails (cut side down) over medium heat until just browned and cooked through, turning once (~ 15 min. total).  Serve hot with the dipping sauce.
crab cakes (makes 8-12)
1.  Gently combine 1 lb. lump crab meat, 1/2 C. diced red bell pepper, 1 minced, seeded jalape
o, 4 t. old bay seasoning, 2 cloves of garlic (minced), 4-6 T. mayonnaise, 1 C. fresh breadcrumbs and salt, pepper, cayenne, white pepper, thyme and chopped onion to taste.  Shape into cakes and chill 1 hr.
2.  Dip each cake in 2 eggs (slightly beaten), then 1 C. breadcrumbs and set aside.  Sauté cakes a couple at a time in 1 T. (each) oil until browned.  Serve with spicey mayonnaise (below).
3.  Combine 2 C. mayonnaise, 6 T. hot chile sauce and 2 T. lemon juice.  Mix well and chill, then serve with crab cakes.

name: Thai crab cakes (makes 12)
instructions:
Gently combine 1 lb. lump crab meat, 2. C. cooked basmati rice, 2 minced, seeded jalape
os, 1 T. lemon zest, 1/4 C. chopped fresh basil and 3 T. chopped fresh mint in a large mixing bowl.  Carefully stir in 2 t. kosher salt, 1/2 t. fresh ground pepper, 2 beaten eggs and 1/4 C. dry bread crumbs.
Form into flat croquettes about 2 in. in diameter and brown in 2 T. olive oil over medium heat in a large, non-stick skillet (about 1.5 min per side).
s **** Beef and Broccoli Stir-Fry (serves six)
1.  Marinate 2 lb. beef strips (thinly sliced) for 1 hr. in 3 T. soy sauce, 2 T. dry white wine, 2 T. cornstarch, 1 t. sugar and 1/4 t. pepper.
2.  Trim and cut into segments one bunch of broccoli, along with four carrots (cut into thin slices length-wise) and 1/2 onion, cut into wedges (optional).
3.  Heat 8 T. peanut (or vegetable) oil in a wok or heavy skillet over high heat.  When very hot, add the beef strips and cook until browned.  Add the vegetables and cook until done (~5 min).  Add the marinade, 4 T. soy sauce, 4 T. cornstarch and 3 T. peanut butter and stir until well mixed and thickened.  Serve over rice.

name: Thai-Fry
instructions:
1.  Bring 1 C. water to boil in a wok.  Add 1 large spanish onion (chopped roughly) and 2 large cloves garlic.  Cook until onion is soft.
2.  Add 2 t. ground ginger (or use fresh), 2 T. crunchy peanut butter, 1 t. yeast extract (or vegetable stock) and 3 T. shoyu (or some ungodly soy sauce).  Cook until thickened, adding water as needed to prevent burning.
3.  Add 1 lb, broccoli and 4 oz diced tempeh (I used organic okara and brown rice tempeh).  Then add 3/4 lb mushrooms, 1 yellow pepper/capsicum, cut into slices and 1 t. black pepper.  Cook over high heat with continuous stirring until done.
Add 1/2 C. corn and cook briefly.  Serve on a bed of brown rice.
(soup) (serves six)
1.  Place 2 lb. oxtails, 3 lb. beef bones (shin or neck) and 1/2 t. salt in 14 C. water and slowly bring to a boil.
2.  Char one halved onion and a 3-inch piece of ginger (halved) in a dry skillet, cut sides down, over high heat.  Turn and char skin sides, then add to stock.
3.  When stock begins to boil, reduce heat and simmer uncovered, skimming off foam that comes to the top.  Continue for 30 min. or longer.
4.  Add 8 whole star anises, 2 cinnamon sticks, 2 large bay leaves, 5 whole cloves, and 1 T. sugar.  Place 2 t. fennel seeds in a tea ball (or tied in cheese cloth)and add to the stock.  Simmer another 3-4 hours.  Strain and pass through a fine sieve.  Refrigerate 3-4 hr. and skim off any remaining fat.
5.  Place 1 lb. rice vermicelli in a large bowl and cover with cold water.  Soak until pliable (about 30 min.) and drain.
6.  Take the stock from the refrigerator and bring to a boil.  Place a handful of vermicelli into a strainer that will fit in the pot, and dip the strainer into the hot stock.  Swirl intil the vermicelli is tender but still a little chewy (about 20 sec).  Shake vermicelli dry over the stock and place in a soup bowl.  Repeat with the remaining noodles.
7.  Divide 3/4 very thinly sliced lean beef, 1/2 C. mung beans, 1/2 C. cilantro leaves, 1/2 C. chopped basil leaves and 2 chopped scallions among the six bowls.  Ladle the very hot stock over the contents.  Squeeze some lemon juice on top and add pepper to taste.  Serve with 3 chopped Thai chiles and bottled fish sauce.

name: Malaysian grilled chicken wings (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Whisk 1/2 C. soy sauce, 3 T. molasses, 1/3 C. sake and 1/3 C. sugar.  Stir in 5 star anises, 2 cinnamon sticks and 2 t. pepper.
2.  Slit 12 chicken wings to the bone, place in a freezer bag and cover with marinade.  Refrigerate overnight.
3.  Grill wings until very crisp, basting with sesame oil.
s Curried Chicken (serves four)
1.  Saut
1/2 chopped onion in 1 T. oil until clear.  Mix a little water with 2-3 T. curry powder and make into a paste.  Add to the onion and sauté briefly.
2.  Blanch four chicken breasts in boiling water, then simmer in milk for ~ 10 min.  Add 1.5 lb. new potatoes (quartered), salt and pepper to taste.  Cover and bake at 350°F until done (~ 30 min).

name: Laksa Lemak Soup (serves four)
instructions:
1. Wrap 1.5 t. of blacan (shrimp paste) in aluminum foil and roast in the oven at 350 °F for 10 min.  Allow to cool.
2.  Discard the tough outer leaves from 2-3 stalks of lemongrass, then thinly slice the lower 6 in. of the stalks.  Fold the upper stalks into thirds and tie into a bundle with kitchen string.
3.  Puree the sliced lemongrass and blacan with 1 T. chopped fresh ginger, 1.5 T. chopped, peeled fresh galangal, 1 t. turmeric powder, 1 medium chopped onion, 6 red hot chiles (Thai or serrano), 2 cloves of chopped garlic, 5 chopped candlenuts (or substitute macadamia nuts
don't eat candlenuts raw!), and 2 T. water.
4.  Soak 6 fried bean curd puffs in hot water for 20 min.  Squeeze dry and halve.
5.  While the puffs are soaking, simmer 2 chicken breasts with bones in 3.5 C chicken stock, turning occasionally until just coked (12-15 min.).  Cool the chicken in a bowl and pour the stock through a sieve into another bowl.  Discard skin and bones, then shred the chicken meat by hand.
6.  Blanch 6 oz. fresh bean sprouts for 15 sec. in boiling water.  Drain well.
7.  Heat 1/2 C. vegetable oil (or olive oil) in a large pot until very hot.  Stir in the pureed spice paste and stir constantly for 8-10 min. (until paste begins to stick to bottom of pot).  Add 14 oz well-shaken canned coconut milk (unsweetened), the stock and lemongrass bundles, and bring to a simmer.  Stir in the puffs and simmer 20 min.
8.  Add 1/2 lb. shrimp (peeled and de-veined) and simmer 1-2 min until just cooked through.  Stir in the shredded chicken and salt to taste.
9.  While the laksa is simmering, bring a large pot to a boil and cook 1 lb. fresh rice noodles for one min., then drain and rinse well (or cook dried noodles according to the package instructions).  Divide the noodles and bean sprouts between four large bowls, add the soup and serve.

name: Chicken-Udon Soup (serves four)
instructions:
1. Soak 1 oz. dried hijiki seaweed in water to cover for 30 min.  Drain and reserve.
2.  Roast 1 broiler-size chicken until done.  When cool, discard the skin and shred the meat by hand.  Set aside.
3.  Cook one 8 oz. package of udon noodles according the the package instructions.  Rinse with cool water until cool and set aside.
4.  Whisk 1/2 C. red miso with 8 C. water over high heat.  Bring to a boil and set aside.
5.  Divide the chicken, noodles, hijiki, 3 C. shredded fresh spinach leaves and 1 C. thinly sliced scallions among four large bowls.  Return miso to a boil and pour an equal amount into each bowl.  Add 1/2 t. sesame oil to each bowl and serve immediately.

name: Spicy Wok Shrimp with Coconut Rice (serves 6)
instructions:
1.  Rinse 1.5 C. long grain white rice, shake well and place in a heavy sauce pan with 1.5 C. water, 1 t. sugar, 1 C. unsweetened coconut milk (stirred well) and 3/4 t. salt.  Bring to a boil, cover and cook 20 min, then remove from heat and let stand 5 min..
2.  While the rice cooks, trim 1 lb. broccoli and cut diagonally into 1-in. lengths.  Toss 2 lb. of large, raw shrimp (peeled and deveined) with 2.5 t. Sriracha sauce and 3/4 t. salt.
3.  Whisk 2 t. cornstarch into 1/4 C. chicken broth and 1/2 C. coconut milk.  Heat a well-seasoned wok over high heat until it just begins to smoke, then add 1 t. vegetable oil, swirling to coat.  Add broccoli and stir-fry until bright green, then add 1/4 C. chicken broth and cook down until evaporated.  Add shrimp and cook, stirring frequently, for 2 min.  Stir cornstarch mixture and add down the side of the wok and boil, stirring until sauce is slightly thickened.  Serve with rice.

name: Japanese Egg Custard (Chawan Mushi) (serves 6)
instructions:
1.  Bring 3 C. cold water and one 8
piece of kombu (dried kelp, ~15 g.) just to a boil in a large saucepan.  Remove from heat and discard the kelp.  Sprinkle one 5-g. package of katsuo bushi (dried bonito flakes, ~1/2 C.) over liquid in pan and allow to stand for 3 min., stirring occasionally.  Strain through a paper towel.  This is dashi.
2.  Whisk 3 large eggs together in a bowl.  Whisk in 1.25 t. mirin (a sweet rice wine), 1 t. ususkuchi shoyu (a light soy sauce), 1/2 t. salt and 1.5 C. of the dashi.
3.  Thinly slice 3 small, fresh shiitake mushrooms and one scallion (on the diagonal); divide among six serving cups.  To each cup add: one medium shrimp (peeled and deveined) and one ginkgo nut.  Divide the egg mixture among the serving cups and top with aluminum foil, and place the cups in a bamboo steamer.
4.  Bring 1
of water in a skillet to a boil over high heat.  Steam the cups for 2 minutes then reduce heat to a simmer and cook until the custard is just set (10-13 min).

name: Grilled Lobster with Vietnamese Dipping Sauce (serves four)
instructions:
1. Combine 4 minced cloves of garlic, 4 minced asian red chiles, 3 T. sugar, 3 T. Vietnamese fish sauce (nuoc mam) and 3 T. fresh lime juice.  Set aside.
2.  Steam 4 1.5-lb. lobsters for 5 min.  When cool enough to handle, twist off claws and tails.  Cut tails in half, lengthwise, and rub the exposed meat with 1 t. canola oil.  Grill claws and tails (cut side down) over medium heat until just browned and cooked through, turning once (~ 15 min. total).  Serve hot with the dipping sauce.
s Chicken (serves 4)
In a small bowl, combine 4 T. tomato paste, 1 t. potato flour, 1 t. dark soy sauce, 3 t. light soy sauce, 3 T. rice vinegar and 9 T. chicken stock.  Set aside.
Skin, debone and remove as much of thesinew as possible from 24 oz. chicken thighs (8-10).  Cut in half horizontally if too thick, lightly score with crosshatches then slice into 1/4-in. slices lengthwise.
Mix 1 t. dark soy sauce, 4 t. light soy sauce and 2 egg yolks.  Add 4 T. potato flour and 4 t. peanut oil, and mix well.  Snip 15-20 dried red chiles into 1/4-in. pieces with scissors, discard the seeds and add to mixture.
Pour 3.5 C. peanut oil into a large wok, enough to be 1.5 in. deep.  Set over high heat until oil is 350-400°F, then fry the chicken in 3 or 4 batches until crisp and deep-golden in color (3-4 minutes).  Transfer the chicken to a plate with a slotted spoon.
Pour off the oil (saving if desired), wipe out the wok and return to heat.  Add 4 T. peanut oil and allow to heat well.  Add the chilies and stir-fry for a few seconds (till they just begin to change color, then add 4 T. finely chopped ginger and 4 t. minced garlic, and cookk a few seconds longer (until fragrant).  Return the chicken to the wok and stir vigorously to coat.  Remove to a serving bowl, mix in the sauce and top with chopped scallions.  Serve with rice.

name: Persian Fried Chicken (serves 8)
instructions:
1.  In a small bowl, combine 1/2 T. saffron with 1 T. water, and let soak for 10 min.  Place in a food processor with 2 C. whole milk yogurt and 1 T. chopped garlic, and puré e until smooth and yellow.
Place 2.5 lb. boneless, skinless chicken thighs in a glass or ceramic bowl, mix in the yogurt, cover and refrigerate at least 3 hours or overnight.
In a medium bowl, combine, 2 C. flour, 2 t. paprika, 1 T. dried mint, 1 T. salt and 1/2 t. black pepper.  Heat a generous 1/2 inch of oil in a deep skillet over medium heat, using a bit of bread to test the temperature (it should bubble vigorously).  Working in batches to avoid crowding, dredge chicken pieces in the flour mixture and frry until golden brown on both sides (~7 min. each side).  Remove and drain on a paper towel.
Sprinkle with salt and serve immediately with 1 C. walnut or pecan pieces and wedges of lemon.

name: Tzatziki (serves 6-8)
instructions: 1.  Combine 1 t. minced garlic, 2 t. fresh lemon juice and 1 t. salt.  Stir in 1.5 C. whole milk yogurt and 1 T. extra virgin olive oil.  Mix well, then add 1 C. grated cucumber or beets (boiled 20 min, with skins rubbed off) and 1 T. chopped fresh dill.    Season with salt and pepper to taste and chill until ready to serve.

name: Whipped Feta with Peppers (serves 8-12)
instructions:
Combine 1 lb. goat
s milk feta cheese (broken into pieces), 2 red bell peppers (roasted with skins removed ,and chopped), 2 t. dried Aleppo chilies, 1 t. dried Urfa chilies, 1/2 t. smoked paprika (or chipotle), 1 t. lemon juice and 1/4 C. olive oil  in a food processor.  Puré e until very smooth, about 2 minutes.  Adjust seasonings, place in a bowl and chill at least 30 min (or up to 2 days).  Serve wih celery sticks and crusty bread.
ITALIAN

name: Fettuccine with shrimp, tomatoes and artichoke hearts
instructions:
1.  In a dutch oven, saut
1/3 C. each chopped onion and carrot in 2 T. olive oil for 2 min.  Add six minced cloves of garlic and sauté another minute.  Add 6 T. flour and cook another 2 minutes with constant stirring.
2.  Add 28 oz. of canned Italian plum tomatoes (coarsely chopped) 4 C. chicken stock,   1 C. white wine, 2-3 t. dried basil and 1/4 t. crushed red pepper.  Bring to a boil, then simmer for 45 min, stirring occasionally.
3.  Add 9-oz package of frozen artichoke hearts and continue to simmer.  Prepare 1-1.5 lb fettuccine on the side.
4.  Add 2.25 lb of uncooked shrimp (shelled and deveined) to the sauce and cook 5 min.  Serve over fettuccine with fresh basil and Parmesan cheese.

name: Turkey Osso Buco
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 350°F.  Heat 2 T. olive oil in a neavy Dutch oven over medium-high heat.  Season 2-3 turkey legs (skin removed) with salt and pepper and brown on all sides (~8 min). Transfer to a serving platter.
2.  Add 1 C. chopped carrots, 1 C. chopped parsnips, 1/2 C. chopped onion, 1 t. minced garlic, 3 sprigs of fresh thyme and 1 bay leaf to Dutch oven. Sauté until just tender (~5 min).
3.  Return turkey and any juice to Dutch oven.  Stir in 1 28 oz. can Italian-style tomatoes and bring to boil.  Cover and bake for 1-2 hr.
4.  Remove and discard bones.  Cube meat and return to stew.   Discard bay leaf and sprigs of thyme, season with salt and pepper.
5.  Ladle into bowl and sprinkle with a garnish of 1/4 C. fresh parsley, 1.5 T. orange peel and 1 t. minced garlic.

name: Turkey with Pasta and Broccoli
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 425 °F.
2.  Trim flowerettes off 1 bunch broccoli and steam for no more than 5 min.  Drain.
3.  Melt 2 T. butter in a saucepan and whisk in 2 T. flour.  Slowly add 2.5 C. milk with constant whisking.  When thickened, add salt and pepper to taste, along with 1/4 t. ground nutmeg and 1/8 t. cayenne.
4.  Add 1/2 C. heavy cream, remove from heat and add 2 egg yolks (stirring rapidly).  Stir in 1/2 C. grated Swiss or Gruyere cheese and set aside.
5.  Prepare 9 oz. fettuccine, place in an oval baking dish and cover with broccoli.
6.  Slice leftover turkey into thin slices and sauté with one crushed clove garlic in 2 T. butter.  Place on top of casserole and cover with cheese sauce.
7.  Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and bake 10 min at 425 °F, then brown under broiler for 2-3 min.

name: Lasagne (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Simmer 2 cans tomato sauce, 1 t. sugar, 1 t. oregano, 1 t. salt, 1/4 t. pepper.
2.  In a separate pan, sauté one chopped onion and 1 clove crushed garlic.  Add 1 lb. ground beef and brown.  Drain and add to the tomato sauce.
3.  Cook 1 package of lasagne noodles per the instructions on the box.
4.  Grate 1 lb. mozzarella cheese.  Layer into a greased casserole dish: the sauce, noodles, grated mozzarella, cottage cheese, noodles, sauce, grated mozzarella, cottage cheese, noodles.  Top with grated romano cheese.
5.  Bake at 375

name: Salmon Lasagne I (serves six)
instructions:
Preheat oven to 425°F.  Halve four red sweet peppers; remove stems, seeds and membranes.  Arrange cut sides down on a foil-lined baking sheet and bake 25-30 min until skins blister.  Immediately place in a paper bag and allow to cool 30 min.  Srip away the skins with a sharp knife.
Reduce oven to 350°F.  Bring 1.5 C. water to a boil in a large skillet., then add 12 oz. fresh salmon fillet, skin side down and cover.  When water has returned to a boil reduce to a simmer until the bright pink flesh has dulled.  Remove from skillet and peel off skin.  Break the salmon into small pieces with a fork and set aside.
Prepare 14 lasagne noodles per the directions on the box.  Rinse and drain twice.
Blend the roasted peppers in a food processor until nearly smooth.  Add 1/2 C. sour cream, 1 T. flour, 1/2 t. each salt and pepper, and 2 T. minced garlic.  Process again until blended.
In a medium mixing bowl, combine 1 beaten egg, 1 C. ricotta cheese, 1/2 C. prepared pesto, 8 oz. cream cheese, the salmon, 1 pkg. chopped spinach (thawed), 1 small onion (finely chopped), 3 T. minced garlic, 1/2 T. fresh oregano and 1/2 t. each salt and pepper.  Mix well.
Lightly grease a 9-in. x 9-in. baking dish.  Arrange a layer of noodles on the bottom, then spread with 1/3 of the salmon mix.  repeat twice, ending with a final layer of noodles.  Top with 8 oz. grated mozzarella.  Cover and bake 50 min.  Remove cover and bake another 10 min.
While the lasagne is cooking, warm the red pepper mixture in a saucepan (or microwave).  Remove lasagne, allow to stand for 10 min., then serve, topped with the pepper sauce.

name: Salmon Lasagne II (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Pre-heat oven ot 375°F.  Cut up one loaf of whole wheat bread and either leave out overnight or dry in the oven on low heat.
2.  Saut
1 large onion (chopped) and 4 stalks of celery (chopped) in 1 stick of butter in  a very large skillet.  Transfer to a large bowl and add the bread, 1 t. dried sage and 2 T. fresh chopped parsley and toss gently till just mixed.  Fold in 1 to 1.5 C. poultry stock - just enough to make the mixture moist.  Add the meat and juice from 2 C. of fresh oysters, and kosher salt and pepper to taste.
3.  Grease a 9-in. x 12-in. baking dish.  Add the mixture, cover with foil and bake for 30 min.  Remove the foil and cook until top is browned (another 30 min. or so).
e 1 C. fresh basil, 4 cloves garlic, 1/2 C. pine nuts and 1 C. grated Romano cheese in a food processer.
2.  Slowly add 1 C. olive oil, pulsing the processor after each addition.  Serve over pasta.

name: Mushroom Bruschetta (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Saut
12 oz. white mushrooms (coarsely chopped), 2 shallots (coarsely chopped) and 3 cloves fo garlic (chopped) in 2 T. butter over moderately high heat, until the mushroom liquid evaporates and the mushrooms are browned (about 10 min).  Season with salt and pepper.
2.  Add 2 C. water and simmer briskly with occasional stirring for 10 min.  Pour the mixture through a sieve, pressing the solids to express liquid.  Discard the solids.  Return the liquid to the skillet and boil down to about 1/2 C. (takes 6-8 min.).
3.  Preheat broiler.  Brush 8 slices of bread from a round loaf with extra-virgin olive oil.  Broil until golden.  Rub both sides of of each slice of bread with halved cloves of garlic.
4.  Saut
1.25 lb. of mixed chopped mushrooms (shiitake, cremini, chanterelle), 2 minced shallots and 3 clov es of garlic (minced) in 2 T. olive oil over moderately high heat with occasional stirring until the mushrooms brown.  Stir in 3 t. finely chopped parsley, 3 t. chopped fresh tarragon, 3 t. fresh lemon zest and salt and pepper to taste.
5.  Spoon mushroom mixture onto toast, ladel on some of the mushroom syrup and serve.

name: Ciamotta (a mixed vegetable stew)
instructions:
1.  Trim stem and leaves from one eggplant, then cut cross-wise into 1/4 in. slices.  Peel 1 lb. "white waxy" potatoes and slice into1/8 in. pieces.  Core 4 bell peppers and slice into 1/4 in. pieces lengthwise.  Peel and core 1 lb. tomatoes and crush well by hand.
2.  Fry the eggplant in 1/8 in. deep olive oil  and place in a large casserole.  Add more oil if necessary and fry the potato slices, then sauté the peppers until barely soft.
3.  Preheat oven to 375°F.  Top the casserole with the crushed tomatoes and two well-crushed cloves of garlic.  Add salt/pepper to taste, mix well and bake 30 min.  Serve topped with Parmesan cheese.
(serves six)
1.  Sauté one chopped onion, carrot and celery stalk in 3 T. butter, 2.5 T. olive oil and 6 strips of bacon.  When soft., add 3/4 C. pork, 3/4 C. beef, 1/4 C. sausage and 2-3 chopped chicken livers.  Add 1/3 C. dry white wine and cook down.
2.  Combine 4 T. tomato paste and 1.25 C. beef buillon, then add to the stew and cook 1-2 hr.  Add 4 T. milk.
3.  Saut
1/4 lb. mushrooms in 3 T. butter with one clove of garlic and 1/4 C. chopped parsley.  Add to sauce at the last moment, and serve immediately over spaghetti.

name: Cassatine di Ricotta (a ricotta cheese pie)
instructions:
Place 2 C. sifted flour in a bowl.  Add 2 egg yolks, 2 T. sugar and 1/4 t. salt and stir.  Add 4 oz. butter in several pieces and 2 T. ice water.  Mix quickly with a wooden spoon, adding more water if necessary.  Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least one hour.
Preheat oven to 350°F.  Toast 1 C. slivered almonds until lightly brown, then raise oven temperature to 450
F.
Warm 2 T. dark jamaica rum and 1/2 C. golden raisins in a saucepan.  Add water to cover raisins and bring to a boil over high heat and set aside for raisins to plump up.
Beat 1 lb. ricotta in a bowl until fluffy.  Add 2 egg yolks with constant beating, then 1/2 C. sugar.  Fold in the almonds and the drained raisins.
In a separate bowl, beat two eggwhites until stiff and gently fold into the ricotta mixture.
Roll out the pastry as thinly as possible and place in each of two greased 9-in. springform pans.  Fill with the ricotta mixture, place on a cookie sheet and bake 20-30 min until golden brown.  Serve lukewarm or cool.

name: Calzones (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Mix 2 pkg yeast w/ 2 C. warm water; allow to sit 5 min. (or use sourdough starter).
2.  Add 3 C. flour and mix well.  Blend in 1/2 C. sugar, 1 t. salt, one egg, and 2/3 C. shortening.
3.  Knead in just enough flour to make the dough manageable and let rise (covered) in a greased bowl until doubled in volume.
4.  Mix 1 lb. ground beef, 1/2 lb. sausage, 1/ 2lb. ground veal, 1 chopped onion, 1/4 t. each sage and thyme, salt and pepper to taste, 2 beaten eggs, 1/2 C. milk, 1 C. bread crumbs (or cracker meal) and 1/ 2C. chopped parsley.  Shape into 2-in. balls, and brown on all sides in 6 T. butter, then cover and simmer 30 min, turning occasionally.
5. Simmer 4 cans tomato sauce, 2 t. sugar, 2 t. oregano, 2 t. salt, 1/2 t. pepper.  Grate 1 lb. mozzarella cheese.
6.  Punch down the dough and roll out into 8-in. circles ~ 1/4 in thick.  Place the dough in a small, well-greased bread pan.  Place some of the mozzarella in the dough, add two or three of the meatballs, a small amount of crea cheese and some of the sauce.  Pull the dough over the contents and seal, wettingthe edges with water.
7.  Turn out onto a greased cookie sheet.  Bake at 375°F for 20 min, or until brown.  Top with moer of the sauce and serve hot.
mon bon calzone (serves six)
1.  Make the dough for Debbie
s no-fail bread, using olive oil instead of shortening.
2.  Char the skin off one red bell pepper (place in a paper bag for a few minutes before removing the skin).  Peel, seed and slice into thin sections. [This is optional]
3.  Saut
1.75 lb. sliced red onions until brown in 2 T. olive oil [This is optional].  Remove, and sauté four Italian sausages (casings removed) until the meat is browned.
4.  Mix 3 C. grated mozzarella and 12 oz. ricotta cheese with 4 t. dried oregano.  Season with salt and pepper.

name: Pre-heat oven to 400
instructions:
balls of the bread dough into 9
rounds.  Stuff with 1/3 C. of the cheese mixture, sauted onions, sausage, more cheese and some bell pepper.  Add a few spoonfuls of prepared spaghetti sauce, fold dough over and seal by pinching shut, and pierce tops with a knife.  Place on a lightly floured cookie sheet and bake 30 min.  Serve with more spaghetti sauce on the side.

name: Risotto (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  Rinse and drain 2 C. converted rice.
2.  Saut
1/2 C. finely chopped onion in 4 T. olive oil, with stirring until browned.  Add 1/2 C. finely minced prosciutto and cook another minute.  Add the rice and cook over high heat, occasionally stirring and scraping the bottom, about 5 min. until rice is browned.
3.  Add 1.33 C. dry white wine and 1/4 t. dried hot pepper flakes with stirring, and cook until the wine is almost fully absorbed (~ 4 min).  Add 1 C. of boiling chicken broth and cook another 2 min., then another 1 C. broth, raising the heat and stirring gently so that the liquid is evenly absorbed.
4.  Add another 1.5 C. broth, salt to taste, and 2 t. saffron blended with 1/4 C. warm water.  Cook over high heat for 6-7 min, stirring occasionally.  Stir in 4 T. butter, remove from heat and fold in 1 C. grated parmesan cheese.

name: Veal Scaloppine (serves four to six)
instructions:
1.  Heat 1/2 C. hazelnuts in a dry skillet over high heat until lightly toasted, shaking the pan frequently.  Cool and peel off the skins.  Set aside.
2.  Dredge 1.5 lb. veal scaloppine (pounded very thin) in flour, shake off excess and sauté individually in butter until lightly browned (1-2 min per side).
3.  Add 2/3 C. dry white wine to the pan and cook, scraping up browned bits from the scaloppine, until the wine has evaporated.  Stir in 1 T. butter, sprinkle the scaloppine with salt and pepper and add back to the pan, turning 2 or 3 times to rewarm.  Remove from heat and add 1 T. balsamic vinegar.  Return scaloppine to the serving platter and serve immediately.

name: Zappa Spaghetti (serves eight)
instructions:
1.  By hand, mix 2 lb. ground beef, 1 C. breadcrumbs, 1/2 C. grated Parmesan, 2 T. fresh basil, 2 T. chopped parsley, 1 t. kosher salt, 1/2 t. black pepper, 2 cloves minced garlic and 2 eggs.  Pack into balls 2 in. in diameter.
Heat 3 T. olive oil in a heavy pot over medium-high heat.  When shimmering, add meatballs a few at a time and brown well on all sides.  Drain and reserve.
Sprinkle 1 lb. pork spare ribs and 1 lb. chuck roast (or blade steak) with salt and pepper.  Add 3 T. more oil to the meatball pot and brown the meat well.  Drain.

name: Lower heat and saut
instructions:
3/4 C. onions in the oil for 3 minutes, add 2 cloves crushed garlic and cook another 2 minutes.  Stir in one 6-oz can of tomato paste and cook until it absorbs the fat, then add 1 t. dried marjoram, 1 t. dried basil, 1 t. red pepper flakes, 1 bay leaf and 1.5 t. kosher salt.  Stir to combine, then add one 28-oz. can of crushed tomatoes, one 28-oz. can of tomato sauce and 4 C. water.  Stir in 1/2 t. sugar, 2 T. chopped fresh parsley and 2-4 pickled peperoncini, then add the meats back to the pot.  Bring to a boil and simmer 2.5 hr.
20 min. before serving, add the meatballs to the pot.  Boil spaghetti according to package directions, then drain and return to pan.  Add 3 C. of sauce and toss pasta over low heat to coat with sauce.
Place spaghetti on a large platter.  Top with 2 C. more of sauce and the meats (slicing the large pieces).  Serve with side bowls of sauce and Parmesan cheese.
MEXICAN/TEX-MEX

name: Jeff Master
instructions:
s four-star chile verde (serves six)
1.  Burn the skins off 2 doz. anaheim chiles.  Blend with six tomatillos.
2.  Saut
2 spanish onions, 6 cloves of garlic , 16 jalape
os and (optional) 2 habaneros or serrenos chiles in 4 T. olive oil.
3.  Grill and cube 4 lb. pork.
4.  Add all the above to a large pot with 24 oz. chicken broth, and simmer several hours.

name: Alternate Chile Verde I (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Saut
3 lb. ground pork shoulder and 2 C. chopped onions in 1/4 C. olive oil, breaking up the pork as it cooks.  Add 6 cloves of chopped garlic and set aside.
2.  Melt 1/4 C. butter in a saucepan.  Add 1/4 C. flour and make a roux.  Cook until deep brown in color, then stir in the pork mixture, 16 oz. prepared salsa verde, 14 oz. canned diced tomatoes, 1 C. chicken broth, 16 oz. diced mild chiles (canned?), 2 minced jalape
os, 1.5 T. oregano, 1 t. cumin, salt and pepper to taste.  Bring to a boil and simmer until thickened, stirring occasionally (about 30 min.).
3.  Remove from heat.  Stir in 1/2 C. chopped green onions and 1/2 C. chopped cilantro. Serve topped with a dollop of sour cream and/or avocado slices and either tortillas or cornbread on the side.

name: Alternate Chile Verde II
instructions:
1.  Light a charcoal grill.  When coals have died down place a large pan of water next to them.
2.  Place a 4 lb. brisket on the grill directly above the water.  Cover and smoke 12-14 hr (adding coals as necessary).
3.  Toast 4 t. dried oregano and 1 t. ground cumin in a small skillet.  Remove and add 4 lb. Anaheim or chiles (roasted, seeded, deveined and peeled), 8 cloves of garlic (roasted) and 3 large jalape
e.
4.  Cover one side of brisket with salsa verde, top with thinly sliced jalape
os, wrap in foil and continue smoking another 30 min.

name: Mexican table sauce (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Blend 2 C. drained canned tomatoes to a coarse paste in a food processor.  Add one small, chopped red onion, and 1/4 C. chopped fresh coriander.  Add chopped, canned jalape
os, salt and pepper to taste.  Serve chilled.

name: Pico de Gallo
instructions:
Combine 1 C. chopped jalape
os (with seeds), 1 C. chopped red onion, 1 C. finely diced tomatoes, 1/4 C. lime juice, 1 t. olive oil, salt and pepper to taste, and 6 T. finely chopped coriander.  Blend well and refrigerate.
When ready to serve, add 1 diced avocado.

name: Guacamole (serves eight)
instructions: 1.  Halve, seed and scoop out the meat of 6 avocados.  Place in a wide bowl with one finely chopped onion, 6 T. lemon juice, 2 t. or more salt, one crushed clove of garlic and 1 t. mayonnaise (NO MORE!).  Coarsely mash up with a fork.

name: Salsa Ranchera
instructions:
1.  Combine 1 tomato, 2 broiled serrano chiles (charred and peeled) and 1 clove garlic.  One seeded and destemmed habanero chile can be added.  Blend coarsely.
2.  Sauté one thick slice of onion (chopped finely) in peanut or safflower oil until soft but not browned.  Add the blended ingredients and cook over brisk flame for 5 min.

name: Fruit/Chile Mole
instructions: 1.  Detem and deseed, then dry-roast 2 oz. dried cascabel chiles, 4 oz. dried ristra chiles, 1 oz. chipotle chiles and 4 oz. ancho chiles in a 250

name: F oven for 5 min, shaking once or twice.
instructions:
2.  Simmer the chiles in 2 qts. water for 30 min.  Drain, reserving 1 C. of the water and the chiles, and cool.
3.  Heat a skillet over medium heat and blacken 1/2 lb. Roma tomatoes.  Combine with chiles and 2 cloves roasted garlic, 1.75 C. fresh pineapple, 1/2 lb. ripe bananas, 1 large Granny Smith apple (chopped), 1.5 t. cinnamon, 1 T. cider vinegar, 1 pinch of ground cloves, 1/4 t. allspice, 2 t. salt, 2 T. sugar and 3 T. peanut oil.  Puré e in a food processor and strain.
4.  Heat a high-sided skillet until it smokes, add the mixture and cook 3-4 min., stirring continuously.  Serve with grilled meat of any sort.

name: Mexican Clam Coctel
instructions:
Open and reserve both the meat and the juice to 8 dozen littleneck or cherrystone clams.
Combine clams with 6 T. finely chopped onions, 3 t. finely chopped garlic, 3 T. chopped jalape
os (with seeds), 6 T. lime juice, 6 T. olive oil and 3/4 C. coriander (fresh, chopped).

name: Nogales Chuck Steak Tacos (serves eight)
instructions:
1. Peel 8 cloves of garlic and sprinkle wth 1 t. kosher salt on a cutting board.  Chop and mash with the flat side of the knife until its a smooth paste.  Place in a food processor with another 2 t. salt, 2/3 C. lime juice, 1/3 C. orange juice, 3 T. tequila, 3 T. ground red chiles (ancho or New Mexico), 1 C. chopped cilantro, 1 T. chopped fresh oregano, 1 T. coarsely ground black pepper and 1/3 C. olive oil, then pulse until just combined.
2.  Place 2 1-in. thick chuck steaks (~ 3 lb. of meat) on a non-reacting dish and pierce deeply all over with a large fork.  Coat both steaks with the marinade, then place one steak back on the plate, top with ~1/2 the marinade, place the other steak on top and add the remainder of the marinade.  Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 8 hr., turning steaks occasionally.
3.  Remove the steaks from the marinade, pat dry, salt lightly and grill over a hot charcoal grill, covered, for 6-8 min. on a side.  Remove and allow to stand ~10 min.
4.  Cut the steaks across the grain into 1/4-in. strips.  Place on a platter and serve with warm tortillas, sour cream,  salsa, grated cheese, chopped green chiles, guacamole.
eta (serves four)
1. Simmer 1/2 lb. dried black beans (soaked in water overnight and drained) in 2 C. water with 1 bay leaf for 1.5 hr (add water as neded to keep the beans barely covered).
2.  Saut
1 diced onion in 2 t. olive oil over medium heat for 5 min.  Add 4 minced cloves of garlic and cook another 1 min., then stir in 1/2 t. ground cumin and 2 T. chopped fresh oregano; cook another few minutes, then remove from heat.
3.  When the beans are cooked, stir in the onion mixture, 1 t. salt and pepper to taste.  Cook another 5 min, then cool and puré e in a food processor.  Scrape into a serving bowl and garnish with sprigs of cilantro.

name: Pork Chops with Tomatillo Sauce (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Combine 2 T. kosher salt, 2 t. ground cumin and 1/2 t. cayenne with 2 C. water.  Stir to dissolve the salt, then immerse 4 boneless porkchops - refrigerate for ~ 1 hr.
2.  Blister 8 medium tomatillos and 1 poblano chile.  When cooled, discard tehskin stem and seeds.
3.  Fry 2 slices of bacon in a sauté pan until crisp.  Drain the bacon.  Saut
2 t. minced garlic and 1 large white onion (diced) in the bacon fat until soft (~ 4 min).  Set aside.
4.  Puree the tomatillos and poblano in a food processor.  Add the bacon, garlic and onion mixture, 1 C. fresh cilantro (stemmed), 1/2 t. brown sugar, 1/2 t. kosher salt and 1/4 t. pepper.  Proces until smooth.  Transfer to a sauté pan and bring to a simmer (add a little water if the sauce seems too thick).  Keep warm.
5.  Remove pork chops and pat dry with paper towels.  Lightly brush with olive oil and grill over medium heat, turning once (~ 5 min on a side).
s Atomic Green Chile Enchiladas (serves six)
1.  Peel and dice 4 avocados.  Add 8 oz. green chile sauce, 8 oz. of sour cream and warm on the stove.
2.  Broil four chicken breasts.  Allow to cool, then shred the meat.  Grate 1 lb. Monterey jack cheese.
3.  Warm a package of tortillas individually, cover each with sauce, add meat and cheese, and roll up.  Place in a greased casserole and cover with the remainder of the sauce.  Bake 15 min. at 325°F until the cheese is melted.  Serve with sour cream.

name: Goat Cheese and Black Bean Tortillas (makes eight)
instructions:
Preheat oven to 325°F.  Mix 12 oz. fresh goat cheese (at rm. T.), 1 T. fresh epazote or cilantro, 4 chopped scallions (inculding green part) and salt to taste.  Stir well.
Fry 8 corn tortillas in hot vegetable oil, draining well.  Fill the tortillas with the above and place, seam down, in a greased baking pan.
Over high heat, cook 1.5 C. cooked black beans, 1 stemmed and minced serrano chile and 1/2 bottle of dark beer in 2 T. vegetable oil.  Simmer for 5 min.
Puré e half the bean mixture until smooth, then return to the rest.  Top the stuffed tortillas with 2 C. of the black bean sauce and pico de gallo.  Warm in oven and serve.

name: Grilled Chicken with Melon Salsa (serves four)
instructions:
1. Toss 1 C. diced honeydew melon, 1 C. diced canteloupe, 1 t. grated fresh ginger, 3 T. minced scallions, 1 t. seeded and minced jalape
o and 3/4 t. salt in a mixing bowl.  Refrigerate until cold.
2.  Grill chicken breasts and serve, topped with the above salsa.

name: Red Chile con Carne (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Boil 1/2 lb. each of ground beef and sausage until cooked.
2.  Saut
1 chopped onion, 2 minced cloves of garlic and 4 chopped jalape
os until onion clear.  Add 1 can tomato sauce, 1 can stewed tomatoes, 1/2 t. cayenne, 1/2 t. Tabasco, 2 t. cumin, 1 t. oregano, 1/2 t. sage, 1/2 t. powdered ginger and 1 can pinto or black beans.  Cook 2-4 hrs, or overnight in a crockpot.

name: Chile con Carne
instructions:
1.  Trim and cube 5 lb. lean chuck steak, then brown in 1/2 C. olive oil.
2.  Mix 1/2 C. flour and 1/2 C. chile powder; sprinkle over the meat while stirring.  Add 2 t. ground coriander, 2 t. ground cumin, 2 t. dried oregano and 3 T. minced garlic.  Add 5 C. chicken broth and cook over low heat at least 4 hr, stirring occasionally.

name: Arty Pork and Guacamole Tacos (makes eight)
instructions:
1. Season 1.5 lb. thinly sliced pork loin (preferably left over) with salt, then sauté over high heat in small batches in 1/4 C. olive oil until very crisp (about 6 min on a side).  Slice the pork into thin strips and set aside, keeping warm.
2.  Saut
1 C. chopped pecans and 2 ancho chiles (stemmed, seeded and finely chopped) in 1 T. butter for 3-5 min.
3.  Stuff 8 warmed flour tortillas with guacamole, pork and pecans, then stuff yourself.
Note:  The guacamole can have the onions sauteed rather than fresh. Also, serrano chiles or crushed garlic can be added to the saut
.  Cilantro can also be added fresh.

name: Russ' Chile
instructions:
1.  Boil 2 lb. lean ground beef in water to cover with 4 T. chile powder.  Drain & blot dry.
2.  Sauté one finely chopped red onion, 4 cloves chopped garlic, 4 jalape
os, (1 chopped habanero), 1 chopped bell pepper, 4 T. chile powder and 2 T. curry mix.
3.  Add one can stewed tomatoes,  one can tomato sauce, one can kidney beans and one can black beans.  Simmer at least four hours. (Try chicken broth instead and another can of stewed tomatoes instead of the tomato sauce).

name: Mexican Beans
instructions:
1.  Rinse and drain 2.5 C. pinto beans.  Place in a pot and cover with 12 C. cold water.  Bring to a boil and simmer for 1 hr.  Add salt to taste and 3/4 C. chopped onions.  Simmer another 30 min.
2.  Saut
3/4 C. chopped onions and 1/4 C. chopped jalape
os in 2 T. vegetable oil.  Add 1.5 C. diced tomatoes and 3/4 C. chopped coriander, and stir over heat for 4 min.
3.  Pass 1 C. of the beans through a ricer and return to the main pot.  Add the tomato mixture and cook over low heat another 5 min.

name: Refried Beans
instructions:
Drain and rinse 1.5 C. dried black beans (soaked at least 8 hr).  Place in a medium saucepan, cover with water and simmer gently for 3 hr.
Drain beans and allow to cool, then mash with a wooden spoon.

name: Cilantro Rice (serves 6)
instructions:
1.  Bring 1.5 C. rice, 2 C. water and 1.5 t. salt to a boil in a heavy saucepan.  Cover with a tight-fitting lid and cook 20 min.  Remove from heat and allow to stand, covered, for 5 min.  Fluff with a fork.
2. While rice is cooking, puré e 1 C. loosely packed cilantro, 1/2 C. chopped white onion, 1/4 C. chopped scallions, 1.5 T. chopped jalape
o (including seeds), 1.5 T. fresh lime juice and 1 T. olive oil.  (Add 1-2 T. water to facilitate blending, if necessary).  Combine with rice and serve.

name: Red Beans and Rice (serves 12)
instructions:
1.  Combine in a large pot: 3 15-oz. cans of kidney beans (rinsed and drained), 2 15-oz. cans low-salt chicken broth, 1 15-oz. can diced tomatoes (with juice), 1 lb. fully cooked beef sausage (diced), 2 C. sliced mushrooms, 1.5 C white and wild rice blend, 1 C. chopped onion, 1 chopped green pepper, 1/2 C. chopped jalape
o (from jar), 1/4 C. A1 steak sauce, 2 T. Worcestershire sauce and 3 cloves chopped garlic.  Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer, cover and cook 35 min. with occasional stirring.

name: Torta de Masa
instructions:
1.  Preheat oven to 375
C.
2.  Butter a 9"x5"x3" pan and line bottom with parchment paper.
3.  Sift 1 C. masa harina, 1.5 t. baking powder, 8 T. sugar and salt to taste.  Cut in 10 T. butter and mix well, then mix in 1/2 C. cold water.  Beat in 6 egg yolks.
4.  Beat 6 eggwhites until stiff and fold into masa.  Scrape into pan and bake 45 min.

name: Fresh Corn Tamales
instructions:
1.  With a sharp knife, remove the kernels from 8 ears of corn and puré e in a food processor.
2.  Place the puré e in a large bowl and add 1/2 C. cornmeal, 1 T. sugar, 2 eggs and 1/2 t. baking powder.  Add 4 T. butter in small amounts and beat until smooth.
3.  Fill each of six large cornhusks with the corn mixture.  Cover with a smaller cornhusk and fold the edges over to cover.  Tie off with thin strips of cornhusk, folding over the bottom.  Holding upright, tap down mixture, top off and tie top.
4.  Place in a vegetable steamer and steam for 30 min.
s Soft Taco Meat (serves six)
1.  Boil 1 lb. ground beef in 2 C. salted water with 3 T. chile powder and 1/2 chopped onion until meat is cooked.  Drain through a strainer.

name: Red Chile Salmon Hash
instructions:
1.  Place 2 lb. salmon fillet (boned) in a food processor fitted with a metal blade and pulse for 15 sec.
2.  Blanch 4 baking potatoes (finely diced) in boiling, salted water until cooked (but not soft).  Rinse with cold water.
3.  Combine salmon and potatoes with 4 T. diced red bell pepper, 4 T. diced yellow bell pepper, 4 T. red chile flakes, 2 T. chopped parsley, 2 eggs, 2 C. bread crumbs and salt to taste.
4.  Form into patties, sauté in clarified butter until crispy.

name: Mexican Cheese Soup I (serves 12)
instructions:
1.  Combine 8 T. butter and 1/2 C. flour in a saucepan and cook 10 min for a roux.
2.  In a large saucepan, saut
1/2 C. chopped celery, 1/2 C. chopped bell pepper, 1 medium yellow onion (chopped) and 2 t. chopped garlic partly coverd until the vegetables
.  Add 2 qts. chicken stock and bring to a simmer.  Whisk in the roux, then GRADUALLY add 1 lb. grated jalape
o pepper cheese, allowing each batch to melt before adding the next - STIR CONSTANTLY.  Remove from heat.  Add 2 T. Worchestershire sauce, 2 T. hot sauce and allow to stand, covered, for ~ 1hr.
3.  Return to heat.  Whisk in 2 C. heavy cream, kosher salt and pepper to taste, stirring constantly - DO NOT ALLOW TO BOIL.  when heated, serve with tortilla chips.

name: Mexican Cheese Soup II (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Stem, seed and devein one bell pepper and one poblano chile.  Dice finely.
2.  Saut
1 medium chopped onion till clear.  Add the peppers and cook another 3 min.
3.  Bring 5 C. chicken stock to a boil.  Mix 2 T. flour and 2 T. cornstarch with 3/4 C. water and add to stock.  Boil another 2 min, then add 3/4 C. evaporated milk.  Lower heat immediately, then add the peppers and cook another 5 min.  Season with salt and pepper to taste, melt in 2 T. butter and serve over 2 T. each grated monterey jack cheese and chopped tomatoes.

name: Santa Fe Chicken (serves four)
instructions:
1.  Soak 3 dried New Mexico chiles in water until soft, then stem, seed and slice.
2.  Saut
2 T. minced garlic and 3 minced shallots in 1/4 C. olive oil until brown.  Add the chiles, 1 can cranberries, 1 T. each of basil, oregano and thyme, and 2 C. chicken stock.  Cook 1 hr. or until thickened.
3.  Mix 3/4 C. chopped pine nuts, 1/2 C. bread crumbs and 1 C. chopped parsley.  Lightly flour 4 chicken breasts, dip in 3 eggwhites, then dredge in the pine nut/parsley mixture.  Sauté breasts in in olive oil, then serve with sauce.

name: Bean Burritos
instructions:
1.  Warm refried beans (above) and 12 large flour tortillas.  Fill each tortilla with beans, top with grated Monterey Jack cheese and fold up.
2.  Mix 1 C. diced red and green peppers, 1 C. diced tomato, 1/2 cup corn and 1/2 C. chopped scallions.  Top burritos with vegetable mix along with sour cream.

name: Pork Chimichangas (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Brown 1 lb. lean boneless pork butt (cut into 1.5-in pieces) covered in 4 T. butter for 10 min.  Uncover, raise to high heat, and cook with stirring until the liquid has evaporated.  Add 2 C. water and stir well.  Cover and simmer until meat is tender.  Uncover and cook down the liquid again.
2.  Stir in 3 T. canned green chiles, 2 T. white vinegar, 1 clove of crushed garlic, 1/4 t. oregano & 1/4 t. cumin.  Cook 5 min., then cut meat into small pieces w/ scissors.
3.  For each of a package of tortillas, brush both sides with melted butter, spoon in meat mixture and fold all sides over the filling.  Place bundles seam side down in a 9-in. x 13-in. greased baking dish and bake at 350°F for 8-10 min. until browned.
4.  Combine 2 medium tomatoes (quartered), 1/2 chopped onion, 3 T. canned diced chiles, 4 t. white vinegar and 1 T. chopped fresh cilantro in a food processor and puree until smooth.  Season with salt and refrigerate.
5.  Top with shredded lettuce, 1/2 lb. grated monterey jack cheese, the salsa and/or guacamole.

name: Empanadas
instructions:
1.  sift 3 C. flour, 2 T. sugar and 1 t. salt into a mixing bowl.  Add 1/2 C. vegetable oil and mix well..  Add 2 lightly beaten eggs, 1/3 C. dry white wine and stir, then knead until smooth.  Refrigerate at least one hour.
2.  Add 1/4 C. vegetable oil to a large skillet.  Over medium heat, sauté one choppeed onion, one chopped green pepper (stemmed and seeded), one clove minced garlic, stirring occasionally until soft (about 5 min).
3.  Add 1/2 lb ground beef, 1/2 lb ground pork, 1/4 lb ground boiled ham and stir until browned.  Drain, then add 3/4 C. chopped green olives, 1 t. kosher salt, 1/8 t. pepper, 1/2 C. tomato sauce and 1/4 C. dry white wine.  Simmer partly covered for 20 min, then remove from heat and allow to cool.
4.  Roll out portions of the dough, spoon in filling and fold over to make a half-moon.  Crimp the edges closed with a fork.
5.  Heat vegetable oil (1 in. deep) in a heavy skillet and fry the empanadas 5 min on a side until brown (don
t overcrowd the skillet).  Drain before serving.

name: Mexican Red Snapper (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Rinse and dry 3 lb. red snapper (whole).  Combine 2 T. lemon juice, 1 chopped clove of garlic, salt and pepper to taste.  Rub on fish and marinate 20 min.  Place in a deep baking dish.
2.  Puree 2 C. light cream with 1 C. fresh, chopped parsley.  Cover fish with the cream mixture and bake 30 min at 325

name: Champurrado
instructions:
1. Chop 2 oz. semi-sweet chocolate and cover with 3/4 C. hot water in a medium bowl.
2.  Heat 1 qt. whole milk and add to chocolate along with 3 T. sugar and 1/2 split vanilla bean.
3.  Whisk in two beaten eggs, then 4 t. cornstarch dissolved in 4 T. milk.  Mix well and simmer for 10 min.

name: Tortilla Soup (serves four).
instructions:
1.  Toast 1 seeded ancho and 1 seeded guajillo chile in a dry sauce pan.  Cover with water in a small bowl.
2.  Fill saucepan with 1/4 in. corn oil and heat over medium heat until oil simmers.  Fry 6 corn tortillas, cut into strips, in three batches for ~2 min. each.  Drain.
3.   Saut
1 small, chopped onion and 4 crushed cloves of garlic in 1 T. corn oil for 5 min.
4.  Blend the onions, chilies and 8 oz. plum tomatoes in a food processor until smooth.  Add most of the fried tortillas and 4 C. chicken broth, and blend again.
5.  Return to heat and season with salt and lime juice.  Ladle into bowls, garnish with avocado slices and the remaining tortilla strips, and serve with a wedge of lime.

name: Mexican Hot Chocolate (serves eight).
instructions:
1.  Bring 8 C. whole milk, six 3-in. sticks of cinnamon, 60 whole cloves and 2 t. aniseed to a simmer.
2.  Add 10 oz. semisweet chocolate (chopped) and 4 T. brown sugar (packed). Whisk until chocolate melts, then remove from heat, cover and allow to steep for 45 min.
3.  Rewarm the chocolate, add 10 T. coffee liqueur (or 2 T. instant coffee) strain into mugs and serve.

name: Cajeta (Mexican caramel sauce)
instructions:
1.  In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 quarts of goat's milk, 2 C. sugar and a 2-in. piece of cinnamon.
2.  When the sugar is dissolved, remove from heat and add 1/4 t. baking soda dissolved in 2 T. water.  Once the bubbling has subsided, return to low heat, stirring regularly until the mixture thickens to the consistecy of maple syrup and is a caramel brown (this can take quite a while).
3.  Strain the cajeta into a storage jar and refrigerate until used.  It can be warmed in a microwave to bring it to room T. (or somewhat warmer) and served with sice cream or fruit.
CREOLE/CARIBBEAN

name: Jerked Pork
instructions:
1.  Combine 8 finely chopped scallions, 1 finely chopped onion, 4 minced cloves of garlic, 3 finely chopped scotch bonnet peppers, 2 t. chopped fresh thyme, salt to taste, 1 t. brown sugar, 1 t. ground allspice, 1/2 t. nutmeg, 1/2 t. ground cinnamon, 1 t. ground black pepper, 2 t. cider vinegar and 1/4 C. vegetable oil.
2.  Marinate 6 lb. pork shoulder or ribs in the mixture for at least 4 hr (ors overnight).
3.  Prepare a charcoal grill, with the coals around the perimeter of the grill.  Place the marinated pork over the hole in the center and grill for 1.5 hr, turning and basting frequently and adding charcoal as necessary to maintain the heat.
4.  Serve with Pickapeppa sauce, corn and roasted yams.
5.  The same marinade can be used to grill three split chickens, 6 lb. shrimp (in their shells), or 18 lobster tails.

name: Jerk Red Snapper (serves four)
instructions:
1. Puré e 3/4 C. jerk sauce, 1 coarsely chopped onion, a sprig of fresh thyme, 1 trimmed scallion, 1 scotch bonnet pepper (stemmed), 1 C. port or sherry and 2 C. water in a food processor.  Pour the mixture into a non-reactive pan and add 4 red snappers (1-1/5 lb. each, cleaned and boned but with heads and tails intact).  Cover and marinate in therefrigerator for 2 hr., turning once.
2.  Place the fish on a fish grid and grill over medium heat until the flesh flakes easily - 5 to 8 min. on a side.

name: Corncakes with Pepper Sauce
instructions:
1.  Mix 3/4 C. flour, 6 T. cornmeal, 2 t. sugar, 1/4 t. salt, 1/4 t. baking powder and 1/4 t. baking soda.
2.  Whisk together 2.25 C. buttermilk, 2 egg yolks, 1.5 T. Tabasco sauce and 2 T. melted butter.  Stir in 1/4 C. creamed corn, 2/3 C. corn kernels (coarsely chopped) and 2 scallions (thinly sliced).
3.  Add the dry ingredients and stir until just blended (the batter can be refrigerated).
4.  Combine 1/2 C. white wine and 3 minced shallots in a saucepan and reduce by half over medium heat.  Add 1/4 C. heavy cream and cook until reduced by half.  Reduce heat to low and whisk in 8 T. butter until emulsified.
5.  Add 2 red peppers (roasted and puré ed), and keep warm over very low heat.
6.  Beat 2 eggwhites and fold into batter.
7.  Add 1 T. vegetable oil to a non-stick skillet and heat.  Add pancake batter in 3-4" circles and cook until bubbles form on the top (2-3 min).  Flip cakes and cook another 1-2 min.  Serve with sauce.

name: Shrimp Creole I
instructions:
1.  Saut
2.5 C. chopped onion and 2 t. minced garlic in 2 T. butter for 5 min.
2.  Add 1 C. chopped celery, 3 chopped bell peppers, 2 minced jalape
os and salt/pepper to taste.  Cook another 4-5 min, stirring often do not overcook).
3.  Add 4 C. cubed tomatoes, 4 T. finely chopped parsley and 1 bay leaf.  Cover and bring to a boil; simmer for 10 min.
4.  Stir in 3-5 T. Matouk's hot sauce (available in Indian groceries) and remove the bay leaf.
5.  Saut
2 lb. medium shrimp (peeled) in 4 T. butter for one min.  Pour the mixture over the shrimp and simmer another minute.
6.  Serve with rice and lemon wedges.

name: Shrimp Creole II (serves six)
instructions:
1.  Boil 2 lb. shrimp in 1 qt. water with 1/2 sliced lemon and 4 black peppercorns for 3 min.  Remove and drain, saving 1 C. of the liquid.
2.  Fry 4 pieces bacon, then crumble into small pieces.  Add 2 T. butter to the bacon fat and saut
1 clove chopped garlic, 1 medium chopped onion, 1/2 C. chopped green pepper, 1.5 C. chopped celery and 1/4 C. chopped parsley for 5 min.
3.  Add the shrimp liquid, 12 oz. stewed tomatoes, 6 oz. tomato paste, 1 T. lemon juice,   1 T. sugar, 1 t. salt, 1/4 t. pepper, 1/4 t. crushed red pepper, 1 bay leaf, and 1/2 t. thyme.  Simmer 30 min.
4.  Stir in shrimp and 1/2 t. fil
powder.  Cook another 5 min, then serve with rice.

name: Corn Pancakes and Goat Cheese
instructions:
1.  Blanch 1 bunch watercress (stemmed) in a pot of salted water for 3 min.  Drain and rinse with cold water.  Pat dry.
2.  Saut
1 T. shallots in 1 t. olive oil for 2 min.  Add 3 T. white wine and reduce, then add 3 T. water and 1/4 C. heavy cream.  Bring to a boil, add the watercress and cook 2 min.  Blend in a food processor.
3.  Place mixture in a clean saucepan and stir in 1 T. peeled, seeded and diced tomato.
4.  Boil 2 ears of corn in a large pot of salted water for 5 min.  Cool under the tap, cut the kernels off and blend well in a food processor with 2 eggs, 1.5 T. flour, 1/2 t. salt and pepper to taste.
5.  Place the blended contents in a bowl.  Heat a frying pan containing 1 t. olive oil.  Add enough batter for one pancake, top with a square of goat cheese and sprinkle with chopped chives.  Turn and brown on other side.
6.  Place two pancakes on each of four plates and garnish with 12 cooked asparagus spears and chervil.

name: Sausage Etouffee
instructions:
1.  In a heavy-duty casserole over medium heat, heat 1 C. vegetable oil.  Add 1 C. flour, sprinkling it in a little bit at a time and whisking vigorously (do not allow to burn).  Continue to whisk until the roux is very dark brown (30 min or more).
2.  Reduce heat to low and add in order 1 C. finely chopped celery, 1 C. finely chopped onion, 1/2 C. finely chopped red pepper, 1/2 C. finely chopped jalape
o, 1 clove minced garlic and 2 T. freshly chopped parsley.  Stir to mix well.  Add 1/2 t. black pepper, 2 t. dried thyme, 3 bay leaves, 2 t. salt and 1/8 t. cayenne, then add 6 C. poultry stock (preferably duck) one cup at a time, stirring well after each addition.
3.  Add 1.5 T. tomato paste and 2 lb. cajun andouille sausage, cut into bite-size pieces.  Raise heat to medium high and simmer uncovered for 30 min, stirring occasionally.  Add 2 lb. coarsely chopped roasted duck meat and cook until heated through (~10 min).  Serve over rice or cornbread.

name: Blackened Chicken Breasts
instructions:
1.  Blend 1 t. salt, 1/2 t. sweet paprika, 1 t. white pepper, 1 t. powdered onion, 1 t. powdered garlic, 1 t. dried basil, 1/2 t. black pepper and 1/2 t. dried thyme.  Rub into each of 8 chicken breasts.
2.  Heat a heavy skillet over medium heat for 8 min, until a spoonful of water dances vigorously on it.
3.  Place the chicken on theskillet two at a time and cook 3 min on a side.

name: Bitter Chocolate and Bananas
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 °F.  Melt 9.5 oz. bitter chocolate and 1/2 lb. butter in a double boiler.

name: Line a loaf pan with parchment paper and brush with butter.
instructions:
Combine 3/4 C. sugar, 3/4 C. brewed coffee and 2 T. flour.  Stir in 5 egg yolks, 1/4 t. salt and the chocolate mixture.  Whip 5 egg whites and fold into the chocolate mixture.
Pour batter into the lined loaf pan and cover with parchment paper.  Place in a large pan and fill the outer pan with 2" of hot water.  Place in oven and cook until set (~45 min).  Refrigerate.
Bring 1.33 C. heavy cream to a boil and add 8 oz. bittersweet chocolate.  Stir until smooth.
Cut 3 large bananas in half, coat with sugar and sauté in butter (both sides) until golden brown.
Remove from stove, add 1/2 C. Kahl
a and ignite.  Allow alcohol to burn itself out then return to stove and reduce over low heat to a thick syrup.
Place 1/4 C. of the chocolate sauce in a plate, lay a slice of the pudding on the sauce, top with the bananas and cover with a little more sauce.  Garnish with sprigs of fresh mint.
2 pounds of chicken breasts
1/2 medium onion
1 sprig cilantro
1 clove garlic
1 teaspoon salt
3 roma tomatoes, chopped
(or 1 can diced tomato)
1/2 medium onion, chopped
2 jalape
o peppers, finely chopped
1/2 teaspoon ground cumin (optional)
1 cup chicken broth*
1/4 cup chopped cilantro, divided
2
avocados, chopped

name: Directions:
instructions: Cook the chicken breasts in 6-8 cups of water with the onion half, a sprig of cilantro, the garlic (crushed), and salt in crock pot  (Medium: 3-4 hours).Let cool slightly.

name: Shred or roughly chop the chicken.
instructions:
Place chicken in large bowl and cover with 2-3 cups of chicken broth (from crock pot).  Let sit over night.
Heat 2 tablespoons vegetable oil in a large skillet over high heat.

name: Saute the onion (add a pinch of salt) until light borwn.
instructions:
Add the shredded chicken (w/ broth), tomatoes, jalapenos, cumin, and 3 tablespoons of the chopped cilantro.
(Note: For the chicken broth, I use the broth the chicken was cooked in.)
Stir to combine; season with salt, if necessary.
Reduce heat to medium-low. Cover and let simmer for about 10 to 15 minutes.
To serve, spoon a couple of tablespoons of filling into the center of warm corn tortillas.
Top with a squeeze of lime juice, chopped avocado, the reserved cilantro and your favorite salsa.

name: Toss 3 lb. wings in 3 T. vegetable oil.
instructions:
When fire is medium-high hot, place wings on the cool side of the grill, cover and cook 15-30 minutes until fat is rendered.
2.
While wings cook, mix
C. sriracha, 2 T. rice vinegar, 2 T. honey, 1 T. sesame oil, 2 T. minced garlic and 1 T. minced ginger
OR 1/3 C. Tabasco sauce, 4 T. melted butter, 1 T. sherry or wne vinegar, 1 T. minced garlic, salt and pepper.
3.
When done, place wings in a large bowl and add marinate.
Return to the hot side of the grill and cook until nicely browned, turning occasionally.
Serve with Ranch
The key is to "render" the wings over low heat first so that much of the the fat melts away.

name: Creole/Caribbean
instructions:
